binaries.title: netio.sys - No security-relevant change: feature-gated audit-mode registry robustness bugfix in KfdStartModuleEx, old path retained
binaries.note: The reported 'changed=1' from the coarse diff undercounts relocation churn but is correct that exactly one function has a genuine logic change (KfdStartModuleEx). An independent content-based diff of both builds confirms all other flagged differences are pure relocation and disassembler-labeling noise.

changed_functions.name: KfdStartModuleEx
key_changes: PATCHED: Introduces a new Windows feature-staging gate Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists (absent entirely from the unpatched build) around the audit-mode second-key handling.

PATCHED (feature enabled): adds a guard that skips the EnableAuditMode query and the WfpRegNotifyCreate registration when the BFE\Parameters\Policy\Options key does not exist (second WfpRegOpenKey exists-flag == 0).
PATCHED (feature enabled): checks the NTSTATUS returned by WfpRegNotifyCreate (test rax,rax at 0x1400A43BC).

PATCHED (feature NOT enabled): the original code path is retained verbatim at 0x1400A43C3, including calling WfpRegNotifyCreate at 0x1400A43E8 with its return value ignored, identical to the unpatched build.

UNCHANGED: the second registry key handle (KeyHandle slot) close behavior is identical in both builds. The unpatched cleanup at 0x1400A43A0 and the patched cleanup at 0x1400A43F8 both close only the first key (Handle slot) via ZwClose. No ZwClose for the second key was added by the patch.
changed_functions.security_notes: The patch does not deliver a security fix. (1) No handle leak is fixed: the second-key handle close behavior is byte-for-byte identical between builds (first key only). (2) The 'unchecked ZwNotifyChangeKey registration return' is benign: WfpRegNotifyCreate (0x14006E5D0) opens and owns its own key handle, zeroes gFwAuditModeNotificationHandle on entry (0x14006E605), sets it only on success (0x14006E6CF), and on failure destroys its own allocation (WfpRegNotifyDestroy at 0x14006E6E6) and closes its own key (0x14006E6F4). Ignoring the return therefore leaves no dangling pointer and no orphaned callback. Moreover the patched default (feature-off) branch still ignores the return, so no NTSTATUS check is delivered on the path that runs when the new feature is not enabled. The genuine delta is a robustness bugfix gated behind Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists with the original behavior retained in the else branch - a staged feature rollout, not a delivered fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED KfdStartModuleEx (audit path + cleanup):
0x1400A4344: jz 0x1400A4395            ; audit off -> skip second open
0x1400A4346: and [rbp+KeyHandle], 0
0x1400A434B: lea r8, [rbp+arg_8]        ; exists-flag out
0x1400A434F: lea rdx, "...BFE\Parameters\Policy\Options"
0x1400A4356: lea rcx, [rbp+KeyHandle]
0x1400A435A: call WfpRegOpenKey
0x1400A435F: test rax, rax
0x1400A4362: jnz 0x1400A4397
0x1400A437D: call WfpRegGetUint32Value  ; EnableAuditMode
0x1400A4382: cmp [rbp+arg_8], 0
0x1400A4386: jz 0x1400A4390
0x1400A438B: call WfpSetAuditMode
0x1400A4390: call WfpRegNotifyCreate    ; return NOT used
0x1400A4395: mov ebx, edi
0x1400A4397: mov rcx, [rbp+Handle]      ; FIRST key only
0x1400A43A0: call cs:__imp_ZwClose

PATCHED KfdStartModuleEx (new feature gate + retained path):
0x1400A4361: call WfpRegOpenKey         ; second open, exists-flag -> arg_10
0x1400A4375: mov eax, cs:Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists__private_featureState  ; NEW gate
0x1400A4384: call Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x1400A438B: jz 0x1400A43C3            ; feature OFF -> retained old path
0x1400A438D: cmp [rbp+arg_10], esi     ; NEW guard: key exists?
0x1400A4390: jz 0x1400A43ED           ; absent -> skip query+notify
0x1400A43B7: call WfpRegNotifyCreate
0x1400A43BC: test rax, rax             ; return CHECKED (feature-on branch only)
0x1400A43BF: jnz 0x1400A43EF
0x1400A43C3: ...                       ; RETAINED old path (feature off)
0x1400A43E8: call WfpRegNotifyCreate    ; return NOT used (same as unpatched)
0x1400A43ED: mov ebx, edi
0x1400A43EF: mov rcx, [rbp+Handle]      ; FIRST key only (unchanged)
0x1400A43F8: call cs:__imp_ZwClose

security_findings.entry_point: Module start; also reached from IoctlKfdResetState (0x140065840) on WFP engine reset via the direct call at 0x1400658A0
call_chain: KfdStartModuleEx (0x1400A425C) - reads BFE registry config, may register an audit-mode change notification
WfpRegNotifyCreate (0x14006E5D0) - opens and owns its own key handle, self-contained cleanup on failure
security_findings.description: The report's original claims are not supported by the binaries. (1) Claimed registry handle leak: the second key handle (BFE\Parameters\Policy\Options, stored in the KeyHandle local slot) is not closed by KfdStartModuleEx in EITHER build - both builds close only the first key (Handle slot) via ZwClose at function exit (unpatched 0x1400A43A0, patched 0x1400A43F8). The patch does not add any ZwClose for the second key and does not add lifetime tracking for it, so no handle leak is fixed. (2) Claimed dangling notification pointer from ignoring the ZwNotifyChangeKey registration return: WfpRegNotifyCreate is self-contained - it opens its own key handle, zeroes gFwAuditModeNotificationHandle on entry, sets it only on success, and on failure destroys its own allocation and closes its own key. Ignoring the return is benign, and the patched default (feature-off) path still ignores it. The genuine change is the introduction of the WIL feature gate Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists (new in the patched build): when enabled it skips the EnableAuditMode query and notification registration if the Policy\Options key does not exist, and checks the WfpRegNotifyCreate NTSTATUS. The original behavior is retained verbatim in the else branch, making this a staged feature rollout rather than a delivered fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No attacker-reachable security defect exists to trigger in either build. KfdStartModuleEx runs at module start and on the IoctlKfdResetState engine-reset path. Because the second-key handle close behavior and the notification registration handling are identical between builds on the default path, repeated invocation produces no patch-introduced behavioral difference.
security_findings.primitive: None. The change does not alter handle lifetime, does not add or remove a bounds/NULL check on attacker-controlled data, and does not touch any memory-safety path. No DoS, info-leak, UAF, or code-execution primitive is present.
security_findings.evidence:
Second-key close behavior identical in both builds:
UNPATCHED cleanup:
0x1400A4397: mov rcx, [rbp+Handle]   ; first key only
0x1400A43A0: call cs:__imp_ZwClose
PATCHED cleanup:
0x1400A43EF: mov rcx, [rbp+Handle]   ; first key only
0x1400A43F8: call cs:__imp_ZwClose
Neither closes the KeyHandle slot (the second key).

WfpRegNotifyCreate (0x14006E5D0) is self-contained (both builds):
0x14006E605: and cs:gFwAuditModeNotificationHandle, 0   ; cleared on entry
0x14006E60D: call WfpRegOpenKey                          ; opens its OWN key
0x14006E6A7: call cs:__imp_ZwNotifyChangeKey
0x14006E6B5: jns 0x14006E6CF                             ; success
0x14006E6E6: call WfpRegNotifyDestroy                    ; failure: destroy own alloc
0x14006E6F4: call cs:__imp_ZwClose                       ; failure: close own key
0x14006E6CF: mov cs:gFwAuditModeNotificationHandle, rbx  ; set ONLY on success

New feature gate present only in patched build:
0x1400A4375: mov eax, cs:Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists__private_featureState
0x1400A4384: call Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x1400A438D: cmp [rbp+arg_10], esi        ; NEW: skip when key absent
0x1400A43BC: test rax, rax                ; NEW: check WfpRegNotifyCreate return (feature-on branch only)
Retained old path (feature off): 0x1400A43C3 ... 0x1400A43E8 call WfpRegNotifyCreate (return ignored, same as unpatched).
security_findings.notes: WfpRegOpenKey (0x14006E848) opens with DesiredAccess 0x20019 (KEY_READ) and sets its third out-parameter to 1 only when ZwOpenKey returns STATUS_SUCCESS; on STATUS_OBJECT_NAME_NOT_FOUND (0xC0000034) it returns success with that flag left 0. The new patched guard keys off exactly this exists-flag. The wrapper symbols WfpRegOpenKey, WfpRegGetUint32Value, WfpRegNotifyCreate and WfpSetAuditMode are the same functions in both builds; apparent address/ID differences are relocation, not distinct functions.

summary: The only genuine logic change between the two netio.sys builds is confined to KfdStartModuleEx (0x1400A425C) plus two newly added WIL feature-staging helper functions. The patched build introduces the feature gate Feature_Firewall_BugFixes_25D_AuditMode_regKey_exists (entirely absent from the unpatched build). When that gate is enabled it (a) skips the EnableAuditMode query and the audit-mode registry change-notification registration if the BFE\Parameters\Policy\Options key does not exist, and (b) checks the NTSTATUS returned by WfpRegNotifyCreate. When the gate is not enabled, the original code path is executed unchanged, so shipped behavior is unaltered until the feature is turned on. The two claims in the original write-up are not supported: no registry handle leak is fixed (the second key handle is closed by neither build; both close only the first key at function exit), and the ignored WfpRegNotifyCreate return is benign because that wrapper owns its own key handle and allocation, sets the audit-mode notification global only on success, and fully unwinds on failure. An independent content-based diff of both builds confirms all other flagged function differences are relocation and disassembler-labeling noise. Verdict: no security-relevant change (feature-gated robustness bugfix, old path retained).
